Promote a character to the framed [CHAR] header the moment they become named, recurring, or important.

The capital of the REGARDEN KINGDOM is the great city built around the base of the Tower, Mercedes Caulis. It is pre-industrial and magic-lit — mana-lamps, enchanted artifices, no machines — and rigidly stratified by magical rank. The inner rings near the Tower hold the noble-mage estates, the Academy quarter, the Capital of Magic stadium, and the public offices of the Tower's factions and the Excavation Agency; the outer rings hold the commoner districts, workshops, and the labour quarters where dwarves and the magicless cluster and are looked down on. Over all of it hangs the false sky. It is the political, economic, and magical heart of the known world, and the stage onto which a fresh graduate steps when they leave the Academy for the Tower.

entries.comment: Location — Capital of Magic & the Grand Stadium
entries.content: The CAPITAL OF MAGIC stadium is the vast arena where the Grand (Great) Magic Festival is staged. It has tiered seating for huge betting crowds, athlete waiting rooms, and two contest spaces: a broad SKY-FIELD overhead for the aerial Sky Race (competitors fly on dragons and other mounts — NOT brooms; Lihanna's dragon-handling shines here) and a ground arena for the team CROWN ATTACK. It is where reputations are made in public, where Will fights alongside Colette and Sion, and where he ultimately defeats Julius before the whole capital.

Beyond its dungeon, REGARDEN ACADEMY is a sprawling campus: lecture halls for its divisions (Edward Serfence's Dark Magic & Origins, Workner's Magizoology, Eliza Nosferat's Nightwalker Ecology & Arcane Magic), training fields and duelling yards, the dungeon gate, the headmaster's offices, libraries, a cafeteria (where Lihanna secretly out-eats everyone), and the student DORMITORIES — Will shares a room with Rosti. Notice boards rank the cohort and post dungeon bounties. It is a place of friendship and casual cruelty in equal measure: the magicless Will endures daily slights here while quietly topping every written exam.

entries.comment: Location — The Colorless Garden (Tower First Floor)
entries.content: The COLORLESS GARDEN sits on the Tower's FIRST FLOOR — an eerie, hueless garden, drained of colour, that newly-recruited graduates reach when they first enter Mercedes Caulis (the High Mage Clairie Serah escorts them in after the Bloom). It also houses the laboratories of Director Kreutz Harlon's Upper Institute. The garden's wrongness is an early hint that the Tower is far stranger — and more political — than the dream of the summit suggests. GATE: its deeper purpose is uncovered slowly.

keys: สถาบันชั้นสูง
entries.content: The UPPER INSTITUTE is the Tower's research body, directed by KREUTZ HARLON — "the Mad Dissector" — with its labs in the Colorless Garden on the first floor. Officially it studies mages, magic, and dungeon specimens; secretly it is obsessed with Will's Wis, and Kreutz blocked Elfaria from scouting Will so the Institute could keep its claim on him. Expect dissection tables, specimen vaults, restricted wings, and a cold, experimental attitude toward people as subjects. GATE: the Institute's true designs on Will are a gradual reveal.

keys: หอพิธีบลูม
entries.content: The PRUNING CEREMONIAL HALL is the grand venue of the Bloom, the post-graduation recruitment ceremony. Here the Magia Vander and their factions select graduates; in the "Blessing of Color," a chosen graduate's emblem — their GLORIA — shifts to the recruiting faction's colour. It is a solemn, charged place where futures are decided in moments — and where Will and Julius, among six, are passed over at the First Bloom while the politics of recruitment play out above their heads.

keys: โรงตีเหล็ก
entries.comment: Location — Regarden Town, Market & the Smithy
entries.content: The commoner districts of the capital — the lower TOWN — are where ordinary life happens: the MARKET, taverns, workshops, and the BLACKSMITH'S FORGE where Will works part-time for coin and where dwarven artisans ply the craft they are scorned for despite their mastery of steel. It is the world of the magicless and the low-ranked: warm, hard-working, and quietly proud, but pushed to the margins by a society that measures worth in mana. Will's bond with the dwarves and his dwarf-rivaling swordsmanship are rooted here.

entries.comment: Location — The Tower Strata & Faction Districts
entries.content: Inside Mercedes Caulis the world is arranged in numbered STRATA. The eight factions keep their bases and districts up through roughly the SIXTH stratum — the Ice faction's halls of Albis Vina, Thorzeus Fasce's thunder-court, the Light, Fire, Wind, Dark and Earth quarters, and the Watchers' offices — each its own fief with officers and adjutants (e.g. Sarissa Alfeld for the Ice faction). From the SEVENTH stratum upward there are no more bases: a climber must defeat the GUARDS set at each stratum's partition to ascend. The summit holds the five Magia Vander, and the apex holds the Great Barrier's seal and Baal. The Tower is thus both a political ladder and a literal gauntlet.

keys: การแข่งเวท
entries.content: The GRAND (GREAT) MAGIC FESTIVAL is a capital-wide tournament and one of the academy year's biggest events, drawing huge, betting crowds to the Capital of Magic stadium. Its marquee contests are the SKY RACE — an aerial race flown on dragons and other mounts, where Lihanna Owenzaus's handling stuns the crowd — and the CROWN ATTACK, a team capture-and-defend event for which a team must field a high combined credit total (about 21,000). Will is pulled in by Julius's challenge and teams with Colette and Sion; mid-event Sion turns on Will over their past, his emotions fuelling his fire; and the arc climaxes with Will publicly DEFEATING JULIUS — the moment that begins turning the academy's contempt into respect.

keys: ภาคปฏิบัติ
สอบปฏิบัติ
บุกดันเจี้ยน
entries.content: The ALL-STUDENT PRAXIS (the Hands-On Exercise / Dungeon Attack) is a large practical examination in which student teams are sent to conquer dungeon floors under real, lethal conditions — a far cry from classroom duels. Will is invited onto an elite team, and it is here, pushed to the edge, that his "Wis" — loading a borrowed spell into his blade — is exposed to his peers, forcing the academy to reckon with what the magicless boy can actually do. Stakes are life-and-death; cave-ins, monster swarms, and hard choices test who the students really are.

The GRADUATION FINALS are the sixth-year's culminating examination, the gate between the Academy and the Tower. Across its trials the rival-to-ally circle is forged for good — Will, Julius, Wignall, Lihanna and Sion fighting as comrades rather than rivals — and final standings are set (Lihanna graduates valedictorian on a perfect 12,000 credits). Passing the Finals sends a graduate on to the Bloom, where the factions choose who climbs. It is a capstone of everything the characters have grown into, and the launch-point of the post-graduation, Tower-ascent story.

Algard — an enigmatic senior mage who steps to the fore in Season 2. He projects both MENTOR and MENACE: knowledgeable, composed, and clearly powerful, yet his motives never fully resolve. He operates around the Tower and academy's upper ranks. GATE: his true alignment and agenda are deliberately ambiguous (the director framed him as 'both mentor and menace') — never state his intentions as known; let unease build.

Logwell — a middle-aged mage and FORMER Regarden Academy teacher who now serves as AIDE to the Fire-Faction Magia Vander Cariott (Incindia Wiseman). In his teaching days he instructed several notable figures — Cariott, Clairie Serah, Workner Norgram, and Edward Serfence — so he is experienced, dry-witted, and unusually well-connected across the academy and Tower. A steady right-hand figure rather than a frontline fighter.

Bruno — an instructor at Regarden Academy. GATE: in the Bloom/Tower arc he is killed by the Goetia's monster Headless (his head twisted off), one of the brutal acts that exposes the Goetia threat. Treat him as a living member of the faculty until that point in the story; his death should land as a shock that proves even teachers are not safe.

Emma — a young woman caught by the Goetia's Shade and controlled through forbidden PUPPET MAGIC, made to act against her own will. She is a VICTIM, not a villain — her plight shows the cruelty of Shade's outlawed art and the human cost of the Goetia's schemes. GATE: that she is being puppeteered is a reveal tied to uncovering Shade.

Gina — a figure within the Tower (Mercedes Caulis). Canon detail is currently thin; portray her as a competent Tower mage or faction officer consistent with the world's hierarchy, and flesh her out cautiously as the story needs.
Gareth — a figure within the Tower (Mercedes Caulis). Thinly documented in available sources; portray as a Tower mage or officer, kept consistent with the faction order; expand cautiously.

Donan — a named side character in the Wistoria cast whose role is thinly documented. Introduce him consistent with the academy/Tower setting (a student, staffer, or minor mage as the scene requires); keep portrayal cautious.
